description_long: This object is a rectangular black VHS cassette tape, featuring two visible spools with green tape inside, seen through transparent windows on the top surface. The front face displays a white label with handwritten text in Cyrillic, as well as printed labels indicating 'VHS' and 'SECAM'. The cassette has a hard plastic shell with subtle molded details, including a seam running along the edge and a small latch mechanism on one side. The sides are mostly plain, with one end showing a small indented area and the other end featuring a visible tape access flap. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function. This object is typically used for analog video storage and playback in VCRs.
description: A black VHS cassette tape with visible spools, handwritten and printed labels, and a hard plastic shell.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main label with handwritten Cyrillic text, printed 'VHS' and 'SECAM' labels, and the two tape spools through transparent windows.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the thickness of the cassette and the edge of the transparent windows, with minimal labeling visible.
description_view_2: The back view displays the plain black plastic surface with the two spools visible through the windows and a small 'SMA' marking.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the opposite edge, including the tape access flap and the continuation of the transparent windows.
